attacking_style: Pure sprinter who contests intermediate sprints strategically for points classification, typically from center of road with powerful finishing speed. Does not initiate attacks but bridges to breakaways when points jersey requires.
team_tactics: DSM (Jayco-AlUla) prioritizes his points jersey defense over stage chasing, controlling peloton pace at intermediate sprints and compromising other team objectives. Heavy domestique investment for marginal classification gains.
terrain_preference: Flat bunch sprints with traditional leadouts. Exposed on penultimate climbs even in wet conditions that typically favor powerful riders. Pure sprint stages with minimal climbing required.
vulnerability: Gets distanced on even moderate climbs before sprint finishes. Rivals actively block his breakaway attempts to limit intermediate sprint points. Dual-role attempts (intermediate + stage hunting) burn matches that compromise finishing power.
key_patterns: Contested intermediate sprints while defending points jersey across multiple Giro stages, often versus PEDERSEN.
Consistently finished second in sprints (at least 4 times) showing speed but not dominance.
Team DSM controlled specifically for intermediate sprints, revealing classification priority over stage wins.
Opponents tactically blocked breakaway attempts when he threatened intermediate sprint points (MATTHEWS example).
Track cycling background translated to immediate Grand Tour sprint success on debut.
Double-duty attempts at intermediate and finale sprints repeatedly compromised his performance.
form_trajectory: neutral: Won Giro points jersey and stage on debut, but repeatedly finished second showing consistency rather than peak speed. Opponents learned to neutralize his intermediate sprint tactics mid-race.
